channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: China’s Atlas Drone Swarm System: Why India Must Prepare for a New Era of Autonomous Warfare
item.description: China’s unveiling of the Atlas drone swarm system in late March signals a decisive shift in how future battlefields could be shaped, not just in East Asia but across the Indo-Pacific. Developed for the People’s Liberation Army, the Atlas system—reportedly capable of launching and coordinating nearly 100 drones through a single operator interface—represents a fusion of autonomy, network-centric warfare, and distributed lethality in a compact, highly mobile format. For India, this is not a distant technological curiosity but a capability that directly intersects with emerging threats along contested borders and maritime domains.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Jammu based Machfly Aerospace Develops ‘Rudra’ Barrel-Launched Loitering Munition for Pinaka Systems
item.description: An emerging project from an IIT Jammu-based lab in collaboration with Machfly Aerospace (SAP) is pushing the envelope in artillery-launched unmanned systems with the development of “Rudra,” a barrel-launched loitering munition designed for long-range, beyond-visual-line-of-sight (BVLOS) strike missions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Eyes Advanced AAS Radar for P-8I to Boost Anti-Submarine Capability Amid US Tech Restrictions
item.description: In February 2026, the Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) granted Acceptance of Necessity (AoN) for the procurement of six additional Boeing P-8I maritime patrol aircraft, in a deal estimated at approximately ?28,000 crore. Beyond fleet expansion, this acquisition marks a significant technological leap, as the Indian Navy is negotiating the integration of the advanced AN/APS-154(V) Airborne Active Sensor (AAS) radar—an upgrade that could fundamentally enhance India’s ability to monitor submarine activity in the Indian Ocean.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: From Green Pine to INS Anvesh: How India is Building a Multi-Layered Ballistic Missile Defense System
item.description: India has steadily built a layered and increasingly sophisticated ballistic missile defense (BMD) architecture, combining long-range early warning radars, interceptor missiles, and sea-based tracking platforms to counter emerging threats from regional adversaries. The evolution of this shield reflects a transition from imported systems to highly capable indigenous technologies, supported by both land-based and naval assets.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Ultra-Deep Military Caverns: Inside the 100-Meter Underground Fortresses Built to Survive 12,000kg Bunker Busters
item.description: India is quietly executing one of the most sophisticated military infrastructure programs in its history, centered on the construction of ultra-deep underground caverns designed to secure its most critical warfighting assets. These are not conventional tunnels or storage bunkers, but engineered deep-rock caverns embedded within dense geological formations such as granite and basalt. Built at depths exceeding 80 to 100 meters, these facilities are specifically designed to withstand direct strikes from some of the world’s most powerful conventional weapons, including 12,000kg-class bunker busters like the GBU-57B Massive Ordnance Penetrator (MOP), as well as ballistic and cruise missile attacks.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan’s Rafale Fantasy Hits 22: How PAF’s “Shot Down” Claims Ballooned from 4-6 to Absurdity
item.description: In the world of Pakistan Air Force propaganda, numbers aren’t just flexible – they’re elastic. What started as a bold claim of downing a handful of Indian Rafale jets during the May 2025 Operation Sindoor clashes has now magically inflated to a staggering 22. Yes, you read that right. The same force that once touted 3, then 4, 5, or 6 French-made fighters as confirmed kills is suddenly crediting itself with nearly two dozen. And the punchline? This “updated intelligence” supposedly came from “vendors” who waited a full year to whisper the secrets.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan Army Trucks Spotted with Makeshift Woven Mats as “Anti-Drone” Protection – Budget Hack or Total Joke Against Kamikaze Attacks?
item.description: In a jaw-dropping image that’s already lighting up social media, Pakistan Army trucks have been caught rolling through arid terrain with what can only be described as the world’s most low-rent roof upgrade. Perched atop one of the military vehicles is a sagging, hand-tied lattice of woven matting – the kind you’d expect to see shading a roadside tea stall, not defending against high-tech kamikaze drones. While the intention might be to create a cheap overhead shield that forces incoming munitions to detonate early, the execution is so laughably amateurish it feels like a parody of modern warfare.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF Invites Industry and Academia to Mehar Baba Competition?3 for Swarm Drone Radar Development
item.description: The Indian Air Force has launched the third edition of the Mehar Baba Competition-3 (https://indianairforce.nic.in/mehar_baba). The theme of MBC-3 is “Collaborative Drone-Based Surveillance Radars.” The competition aims to encourage innovation in advanced drone and radar technologies. Meritorious participants will receive development funding from the Indian Air Force, and the top three winners will be honored with awards. The registration for MBC-3 is commencing on 27 Apr 26.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: At UN, India pushes for terror tag for TRF
item.description: India has intensified its diplomatic efforts at the United Nations to officially designate The Resistance Front (TRF) as a global terrorist organisation. This push follows the group’s involvement in the deadly Pahalgam terror attack on 22 April 2025, which claimed 26 lives.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: COAS Gen Upendra Dwivedi Inducted Into International Hall Of Fame At Us Army War College
item.description: Chief of Army Staff Upendra Dwivedi was inducted into the International Hall of Fame at the Army War College (AWC), Carlisle Barracks, United States, marking a significant recognition of his military career and leadership, said the Indian Army on Friday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Intelligence Signals Turmoil In Jaish-E-Mohammad As Mystery Around Masood Azhar Deepens
item.description: The mystery around Jaish-e-Mohammad (JeM) chief Masood Azhar is growing with each passing day. His own cadres have been questioning the top leadership about his whereabouts. There are claims within the JeM that their chief is on his deathbed. The terror group’s leadership has been fighting such rumours and has been asking the cadres to calm down and stay hopeful.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Offers Natural Advantage To Become Global Aviation Transit Hub: Ram Mohan Naidu
item.description: India’s unique geographical location between the eastern and western hemispheres provides a natural advantage in emerging as a global transit hub, Civil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu has stressed.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, Qatar discuss ways to boost trade, strengthen supply chains
item.description: India and Qatar on Thursday held discussions on ways to boost bilateral trade and strengthen supply chain resilience, which has been disrupted due to the West Asia crisis.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India approves ?3,000 crore withdrawal to Maldives under Saarc swap
item.description: India has approved the first withdrawal of ?3,000 crore under its continuing economic and financial assistance to Maldives, according to the Indian High Commission here.The funds are being released under the first drawdown of the Saarc Currency Swap Framework, the Indian mission said in a statement on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Six Miscreants Booked Under PSA In J&K’s Sopore
item.description: The Police in Jammu and Kashmir’s Sopore area of Baramulla district said on Friday that it has booked six miscreants under the Public Safety Act (PSA). A police statement said: “The Jammu and Kashmir Police in Sopore have booked six miscreants under the Public Safety Act for involvement in recent vandalism and public disorder. The six alleged miscreants are detained at Bhaderwah District Jail and more involved are being identified and strict action will continue.”
item.dc:creator: admin
